     The Numbers Business Report

The Butterfly Effect (2004)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $57,924,679Details
International Box Office $37,713,913
Worldwide Box Office $95,638,592
Further financial details...

Synopsis

College student Evan Treborn is afflicted with headaches so painful that he frequently blacks out. While unconscious, Evan is able to travel back in time to difficult moments in his childhood. He can also alter the past for friends, like Kayleigh, who was molested by her father. But changing the past can drastically alter the present, and Evan finds himself in nightmarish alternate realities, including one where he's locked away in prison.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$17,065,227 (29.5% of total gross)
Legs:3.39 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:60.6% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$13,000,000 (worldwide box office is 7.4 times production budget)
Theater counts:2,605 opening theaters/2,605 max. theaters, 5.6 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $111,558,646

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: January 23rd, 2004 (Wide) by New Line
International Releases: March 11th, 2004 (Wide) (Australia)
Video Release: July 6th, 2004 by New Line Home Video, released as Butterfly Effect, The
MPA Rating: R for violence, sexual content, language and brief drug use.
Running Time: 114 minutes
Franchise: Butterfly Effect
Plot point: Alternate or Altered Realities, Child Abuse, Dysfunctional Family, Faulty Memory, Pedophilia, Prison, Revenge, Romance, Sex Crimes, Surprise Twist, Time Travel
Subgenre: Psychological Thriller
Source:Original Screenplay
Genre:Thriller/Suspense
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Science Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Film Engine, Katalyst Films, Benderspink Productions
Production Countries: United States, Canada
Languages: English

Synopsis

College student Evan Treborn is afflicted with headaches so painful that he frequently blacks out. While unconscious, Evan is able to travel back in time to difficult moments in his childhood. He can also alter the past for friends, like Kayleigh, who was molested by her father. But changing the past can drastically alter the present, and Evan finds himself in nightmarish alternate realities, including one where he's locked away in prison.
